seoTitle: How to Add a Subdomain in Cloudflare (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Create a custom subdomain like blog or shop in Cloudflare by adding an A record or CNAME alias, then decide whether to proxy it for caching and security.
intro: A subdomain lets you carve out a separate section of your site, like shop.yoursite.com or blog.yoursite.com, without buying a new domain. This guide covers adding that subdomain in Cloudflare's DNS panel, whether you're pointing it directly to a server IP or aliasing it to another URL.

steps.heading: Open your DNS Records page
steps.body: From the Cloudflare dashboard, select your domain and go to the DNS Records section in the left sidebar.

steps.heading: Click Add Record
steps.body: Open the field configuration panel by clicking the '+ Add record' button.

steps.heading: Choose A record or CNAME
steps.body: Set the Type to an A record if you're pointing to a hosting IP, or CNAME if you're aliasing to another domain or platform URL.

steps.heading: Enter your subdomain name
steps.body: Type just the prefix, like 'blog' or 'shop', into the Name field without repeating your root domain.

steps.heading: Set the proxy status and save
steps.body: Decide whether to proxy the record through Cloudflare for caching and edge security, then save and verify the new address resolves correctly.

faq.question: What's the difference between an A record and a CNAME for a subdomain?
faq.answer: An A record points your subdomain directly to a server's IP address, while a CNAME aliases it to another domain name, which is common when using third-party platforms.

faq.question: Should I proxy my new subdomain through Cloudflare?
faq.answer: Proxying (orange cloud) enables Cloudflare's caching and security features, while DNS-only mode (gray cloud) routes traffic directly to your origin without those benefits.

faq.question: How long does it take for a new subdomain to start working?
faq.answer: Changes inside Cloudflare's own network typically propagate almost immediately, though full global DNS propagation can occasionally take longer.

faq.question: Can I add multiple subdomains to the same domain?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add as many A or CNAME records as you need, each with its own unique subdomain prefix.
